resources.string: Today\'s Rest, Charge and Effort, with live heart rate and strap battery at a glance.
NOOP widget couldn\'t draw — it will recover on the next update.

When you open the official WHOOP app, NOOP reads on-screen Recovery % and Day Strain (0–21) so our models can train against the app — not the bracelet alone. No passwords. Turn off in NOOP Settings → Auto-capture WHOOP app scores.
Cycle calendar, Lab Book (cuff BP), and step training — one tap each.
Change NOOP’s language on this phone. System follows the device; other choices force Español, Français, Deutsch, or English.

Make it yours — Light, Dark, or System, app language (English, Español, Français, Deutsch) below, plus named packs (Pink Blossom, Ocean Glass, and more). Change any time; frosted nav comes with packs that enable it.
Strap stream · keeps BLE awake · session HR on the workout card is authoritative mid-set
Please read the points below, then confirm each statement.
NOOP is an unofficial project: not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by WHOOP, Inc. \"WHOOP\" is their trademark, used only to name the hardware NOOP works with.
Using NOOP may breach WHOOP\'s Terms of Service
Use it only with a device you own, to read your own data. Whether to use it (and any effect on your WHOOP account, subscription, device, or warranty) is your decision, and your risk alone.

NOOP talks to your strap\'s firmware over an unofficial, independently-mapped protocol. There is a residual risk to the device, its data, and its connection to official services. You assume that risk.
Not a medical device, not medical advice
Every metric is an unvalidated approximation. Don\'t use NOOP to diagnose, treat, or make any health decision. Always consult a qualified professional.

NOOP is free and provided \"as is\", with no warranty. Liability is limited to the maximum extent the law that applies to you allows, and nothing here removes protections your local law won\'t let us remove.
The full terms are in TERMS.md, shipped with NOOP. This is not legal advice.
I am not a WHOOP employee, contractor, or affiliate, and I am not using NOOP on WHOOP\'s behalf.

I own the WHOOP device I will use with NOOP and will only use it to access my own data. Doing so is my decision and my risk, including any effect on my WHOOP account, subscription, device, or warranty, and it may breach WHOOP\'s Terms of Service.
I understand NOOP is unofficial and experimental, is provided free and \"as is\" with no warranty, and is not a medical device or medical advice.
To the fullest extent the law allows, I will not hold the NOOP project or its contributors liable for any loss or damage arising from my use of it.
I have read and accept these terms, and I\'m using NOOP with my own device and my own data, at my own risk.
Swipe the page · or tap the date
Live HR · optical lock for RMSSD
The method behind the score, not today\'s values.
How hard today has been on your body from open BLE heart-rate work. Vessel and Key Metrics share this number.
NOOP Effort is 0–100. WHOOP Day Strain is 0–21. Compare only after ×100/21.
Number on the vessel is from the WHOOP app (not NOOP open BLE).
Night readiness from sleep you banked on-device. Higher is better — use this for can I train. Open Sleep for hours and stages.
Saves to your profile for Effort and Fuel. Your typed weight updates Key Metrics right away; a newer Health day can replace it later.
load · lower is easier · fills up · WHOOP calls this Strain
higher is better · night readiness · Sleep tab
Goal met · keep sipping if you like
Hydration %1$.1f of %2$.1f litres. Sip adds 250 millilitres.
Tap + menu. Hold + dial: Workout · Live HR · Nutrition · Devices · Water. Journal is secondary in the menu.
Stress here means how activated your body looks vs your usual calm — not mood or anxiety.
Named finish for the whole app. Frosted nav comes with packs that enable it.
Choose which tiles show on your Control Center and reorder them with the arrows.
Day trend · live HR on Health
No heart rate for this day. Step back to a day the strap was worn.
No heart rate in the last %1$s. Try a wider window or Today.
Calibrating — no heart rate banked yet today. Your curve fills in as the strap offloads.
Syncing strap… scores hold until bank settles
Pinch to zoom · drag to pan
Put your WHOOP nearby. NOOP pairs over Bluetooth — no cloud.
Or import a WHOOP export later in Data Sources.
See how Charge, Effort and Rest are calculated, and how they differ from WHOOP.
Show what NOOP is synced from
Past day · scores only from what was banked that wake-day — not today’s live partial sync.
Sparse days · Charge builds over nights. Steps estimate calibrates in Settings → Steps when phone steps exist to compare.
Live now. Your scores are building.
Your live heart rate is working from the strap, and Charge, Effort and Rest build from it over your next few nights of wear, sharpening as it learns your baseline.
Charge, Effort and Rest build over your next few nights of wear.
Charge, Effort and Rest become personal after a few nights of wear.
Wear 1 more night of %1$d · personalize calm
Wear %1$d more nights of %2$d · personalize calm
Each line is how many points that signal moved Charge versus sitting at your on-device baseline. Approximate, not medical advice.
Building your baseline. About 1 more night until your scores are personal.
Building your baseline. About %1$d more nights until your scores are personal.
This is your last scored session. Wear the strap overnight for a fresh score.
Tonight\'s lands after you sleep with the strap on.
NOOP · No data for today. Was your strap worn and connected overnight?
Early estimate · %1$d of last 7 nights — still calibrating
1 new day of history is ready in Trends.
%1$d new days of history are ready in Trends.
No metrics yet. Import your WHOOP export or wear the strap to begin.
Live estimate from available nights — still building weekly bank
Fuel · log meals on-device. Opens Nutrition.
Live link looks like an unworn 5AM sibling. Prefer your worn MG when both are bonded.
No cardio load yet. Effort builds once your heart rate climbs into your effort zone (around 60%% of your heart-rate reserve). A calm day honestly reads near zero.
Warm dark surfaces with NOOP\'s gold accent — the default look.
Cool navy with frosted glass nav.
Charcoal ground, copper ember — not cream paper.
Dusk amber on ink — no purple rest.
Deep slate surfaces with a cool steel-blue accent.
Soft floral pink, frosted glass nav.
Tonight first · then ledger · trends · tools.
Sleep is night-first. Today is day-first. Same body — different chapter.
Ready: strap on · permissions · first night · optional Cycle.
Sleep tracks the night you banked. Alarm is tomorrow’s wake window — not the same schedule.
Wake times use this phone’s local clock. After travel or a clock change, confirm the wake day before arming.
Nights follow this phone’s local wake day. After travel or DST, a night may look long or short — check the date before editing.
DST: tonight may look long or short on the clock — local wake day is still correct.
Your age, size and zones — the same numbers Fitness Age and Charge lean on. Keep them honest; they never leave this phone.
How you like distances, weight, Effort scale and the clock. Stored data stays SI — this only changes what you see. Next to Appearance below.
Strap, Health Connect and alerts in one place — the pipes that keep Today honest.
Pair your WHOOP here over Bluetooth — no WHOOP app login, no cloud in the middle.
When the strap battery is low and draining, ease the load. Off until you want it; never while charging.

Inside the window, a rise from the lowest stable heart-rate readings may cue an early wake. That is a coarse HR cue, not sleep-stage detection. If the strap is not streaming, only the guaranteed end-of-window alarm fires.
NOOP sets a guaranteed phone alarm and can use an early HR-based cue inside your chosen window. It does not diagnose sleep stages.
After you wake, if your heart rate rises then falls again (likely dozing), NOOP cues you once more. Coarse HR heuristic — not sleep-stage detection.
How long to keep watching live HR.
Also fire a phone notification when turn-back triggers (strap buzz always tries).
Inside your window, wake early once sleep need looks met or Charge is already green. Hard deadline still stands.
Solve a short sum before the phone alarm clears.
At the hard deadline, require math + louder cue if live HR is below the drowsy threshold.
Below %1$d bpm at final alarm → math + loud.
Ask about your recovery, strain, sleep and HRV, grounded in your own numbers.
Bring your own API key. It is stored encrypted on this device and only used to send your question plus a short summary of your metrics to the provider you pick.
Bring your own Google AI Studio key. It is stored encrypted on this device and only used to send your question plus a short summary of your metrics to Gemini.
Point the coach at any OpenAI-compatible server: a local model (Ollama, LM Studio, llama.cpp) keeps everything on your device; an API key is optional.
Let the coach use my data
On: your recovery, sleep, HRV and workouts are shared with the provider for tailored coaching.
Off: the coach answers generally and sends none of your metrics.
Ask anything about your recent recovery, strain, sleep or HRV.
Customised. Your edited instructions frame every reply.
Edit how the coach thinks and talks. Takes effect on your next message.
Private by default: only your question and a short metrics summary are sent, and only after you set a key.
The coach talks only to the server URL you set. Point it at a local model to keep everything on your device. Nothing is sent until you ask.
Only if your server requires one
How\'s my recovery trending this week?
Should I train hard or take it easy today?
Why might my HRV be low lately?
How can I improve my sleep?
Enter any model id the provider accepts.
Optical lock… R-R usually after ~30–60s on MG
A still, seated snapshot of your heart-rate variability
Patterns in your own data: association, not cause.
Insights read your journal and outcomes

Start in Journal above, then log a few Workouts. After some days of answers, NOOP ranks how each habit moves Charge, HRV and sleep. A WHOOP export that includes its journal backfills history instantly.
Ranked, lag-aware: which of your habits actually move your Charge, plus your personal alcohol/caffeine dose-response.

Log alcohol or late caffeine with an amount and NOOP fits a personal dose curve: how much each extra unit tends to move your numbers. Until then it shows typical patterns, clearly labelled as not yet yours.
Based mostly on typical patterns, not yet yours. Log a few more %1$s days and this becomes yours.
In your data so far, this doesn’t move your %1$s the way it typically does.
“Dose” here is timing (later in the day = stronger), not milligrams.

Everything here is a pattern in your own logged days: an association with an effect size and confidence, never a cause or a diagnosis. Population patterns are shown as “typical” and are always overridden by your own data once you have enough of it. Approximations, not WHOOP’s scores; not a medical device.
Not enough overlap between your journal answers and %1$s yet. Keep logging. Each behaviour needs days both with and without it before NOOP can read its effect.
No extra tonight. Your %1$s forecast stays where it is.
A %1$s tonight tends to line up with about %2$s %3$s on tomorrow’s %4$s for you, %5$s.
Each extra unit typically lines up with about %1$s %2$s %3$s — typical patterns, not yet yours (n=%4$d).
Each extra unit tends to line up with about %1$s %2$s %3$s for you (n=%4$d).
In your data so far, this doesn’t move your %1$s the way it typically does (n=%2$d).
Dose-response curve. Each extra %1$s lines up with about %2$s on %3$s, %4$s
What each activity costs your recovery
Tag a few sessions of the same activity and NOOP will learn its personal recovery cost.
Pick one behaviour you log, one outcome, and a short window. NOOP compares the days you log the behaviour against your behaviour-free days before the start.
Log at least one behaviour above before starting an experiment.
Collect a few logged intervention days before reading the effect. Baseline and imported metrics stay in place.
%1$s is flat against baseline on logged intervention days.
%1$s is %2$s better than baseline on days you logged this behaviour.
%1$s is %2$s worse than baseline on days you logged this behaviour.
Sessions like this barely move your next-day Charge (n=%1$d).
Sessions like this usually cost you about %1$d Charge points the next morning (n=%2$d).
Sessions like this usually cost you about %1$d Charge points the next morning and take about %2$d days to bounce back (n=%3$d).
Sessions like this usually lift about %1$d Charge points the next morning (n=%2$d).
Sessions like this usually lift about %1$d Charge points the next morning and take about %2$d days to bounce back (n=%3$d).
On days you logged %1$s, your %2$s was no different.
On days you logged %1$s, your %2$s averaged %3$s, %4$s than the %5$s on days you didn\'t.
On days you logged ‘%1$s’, %2$s was %3$s (avg %4$d vs %5$d, n=%6$d vs %7$d)
Not enough overlapping history to correlate your metrics yet.
Heart-rate variability as the engine behind your charge score.
How closely a good night tracks next-morning charge.
A lower resting heart rate usually means a higher charge.
How much one day\'s charge carries into the next.
Proof that the console is current
Encrypted stream · live R-R locked · deep controls available.
Live heart rate is flowing; full strap controls need an encrypted bond.
Radio connected, stream not yet trusted.
Standard HR is not a full bond
Sit still, breathe normally. Keep your wrist relaxed and steady.
Done. Save this reading to keep it in your trends.
Sit still and breathe normally. Tap below to take a 60-second reading.
Connect your strap on the Live screen to take a reading.
Not enough clean beats - sit still and try again.
Your night data is in. Logging how you felt helps NOOP learn what drives your best recovery.
Tap when you\'re heading to bed or when you wake. Each tap is logged with the time. It doesn\'t change tonight\'s detected sleep.
Soft wake window finds a lighter moment · customs are exact phone times · strap buzz is separate. One soft window + optional custom times — not many competing masters.
Exact-alarm access is off, so enabled custom alarms cannot fire. Open system settings to restore them.
No customs yet. Soft window covers nights in; add one for a hard weekend or travel time.
This deletes the fixed phone alarm. Soft wake window is unchanged.
Standalone firmware alarm on the strap (works with NOOP closed). Different from “%1$s” above, which only follows the phone soft window while NOOP is open.
Turn on to set weekdays and per-day wake times on the strap.
Armed on the strap itself with the acknowledged 5/MG command. Keep the phone alarm on as backup for anything you truly can\'t miss.
Connect your strap to arm this; it\'s set on the strap\'s own firmware alarm. Keep the phone alarm on as backup.

Armed on the strap itself, so it can buzz at your wake time even if your phone is asleep or NOOP is closed. Sends the exact alarm command the official app sends, confirmed buzzing on a real WHOOP 4.0 (community wire capture + on-device test, #535). Keep a backup alarm for anything you truly can\'t miss.
Connect your strap to arm this; it\'s set on the strap\'s own firmware alarm. Confirmed working on WHOOP 4.0; still experimental on 5.0 and MG. Keep a backup alarm for anything you truly can\'t miss.
Test one-shot strap buzz. Not wake arm.
Buzz wall-clock time on strap. Not wake arm.
Buzzing wall-clock time on strap (not wake arm).
Reconnect your strap, then tap Test buzz.
Accept the Bluetooth pairing prompt, then tap Test buzz again.
One-shot buzz sent (not wake arm). Watch for a buzz on the strap.
One-shot buzz sent (not wake arm). Watch for a buzz on the strap. Phone deadline still fires alone.
Strap accepted the buzz. If the wrist is quiet, reconnect exclusive and try again.
Strap didn\'t buzz — reconnect and try Test buzz again.
Phone zone %1$s · wake times follow this phone clock after travel or DST.
Strap buzz arms on next connect · Soft wake window on this phone needs the link for early cue.
Phone alarm sound & vibe · system notification settings
Open phone alarm sound and vibration settings
Another custom alarm already uses that name
Alarms wake you · Notifications are system alerts · Cycle reminders are separate.
Soft-window buzz (“%1$s”) needs NOOP open. Firmware (“%2$s”) can fire closed. Phone deadline backs both.
Schedule cue, not a health target.
Record three nights for a personal bedtime cue.
Bleed-window BMR is often near baseline in indirect-calorimetry series; symptom load is individual — awareness only, never rewrites Charge.
Follicular: group-level lower RHR / higher HRV (wearable + BBT cohorts); resting energy often ≈ baseline — awareness only.
Peri-ovulatory: biphasic BBT nadir → rise; REE often begins climbing toward luteal — awareness only.
Luteal: RHR↑ HRV↓ skin-temp↑; REE often +2–7% vs follicular in calorimetry reviews — soft capacity only.
Shows a soft sunrise, day, dusk and night scene behind the Today screen. Turn it off for a plain dark canvas. Your cards stay exactly as readable.
Extends the sky behind the whole Today screen, so lowering Card transparency lets it show through every card. Needs the day-cycle background on.
How see-through the cards (Heart Rate, Key Metrics, …) are. Left = solid, right = clear.
Main sleep = longest overnight block · naps stay separate.
No naps · overnight block above is main sleep
This moves the night to a time with no recorded data. Stages can\'t be derived there, so it may show as empty until data covers it.
Removes this sleep and recomputes the day without it. You can undo for a few seconds after.
Removes this recorded sleep and recomputes the day without it. NOOP won\'t re-detect sleep in this window. You can undo for a few seconds after.
Night metrics for this sleep — not the Trends tab.
Open Trends for Charge · Effort · Rest week view
Open Trends tab for Charge Effort Rest
Hours vs Needed progress bar, %1$d percent
This metric needs at least two nights of data.
Not enough history in this range. Try 3M, 6M, or ALL.
No earlier night stored yet. Earlier nights sync in the morning.
Period tracking / Cycle is the first switch below. Optional wellness signals — off by default, on this phone only, never a diagnosis.
Cycle calendar under More → For your body, Health awareness, and + quick actions. Off by default; on-device only.
Cycle / period calendar — available on any profile (including male). More → For your body → Cycle. Off by default; on-device only.
Hides Period tracking shortcuts under More → For your body. Logged periods stay on this phone; you can turn Cycle back on anytime.
Cycle off · Settings → Health & wellness → Period tracking, or More → For your body → Cycle
Open Cycle or Replay Cycle setup
Master on/off for Period tracking lives only here. Cycle opens the calendar when enabled.
Cycle shortcuts are off — open More → For your body → Cycle (or Replay above). Setup isn\'t lost.
Opens the Cycle welcome flow again next time you visit Cycle. Your logged periods stay on this phone.
Charge is NOOP\'s daily readiness score, learned from your own HRV, resting heart rate and more over time. Your history stays.
Restarts the roughly 4-night build-up for Charge and your HRV baseline from tonight. Use it if a bad first week set your baseline off. Your history stays.
This restarts the roughly 4-night build-up for Charge and your HRV baseline. Your history stays. Use it if a bad first week, like wearing it while sick, set your baseline off.
Charge baseline reset. NOOP will re-learn it from tonight. Your history stays, and it takes a few nights to settle.
Couldn\'t check. Retry · or open GitHub Releases for this build.
Checks GitHub for the latest version when you tap. Nothing else is sent.
Meals with BMR · supplements — on-device only
See fuel and bedtime on Today
Harris–Benedict from your profile — estimate, not medical advice.
Cycle-aware recovery: take it easy — soft Charge capacity ×%1$s.
Log meals · on-device · Macros optional. Stays on this phone — never invents SpO₂/BP.
Catalog — tap to log taken today
Daily ~%1$d:00 · skips if already logged · not medical advice
Creatine, magnesium, and more — local log only, not medical advice.
Ledger = last %1$d nights (slept − need). Same as Sleep-debt ledger.
Phone night window differs from NOOP bed–wake — may not be the same sleep.
NOOP bed–wake is on-device for this wake-day. WHOOP app’s night for the same wake-day may use a different onset even when wake times look close.
WHOOP sleep performance %1$d%% — NOOP Rest still needs a tracked night.
WHOOP %% is sleep performance, not hours asleep — different construct from NOOP duration. Sources → Import to refresh WHOOP history.
Phone side is time in bed (no staged asleep in HC) — not comparable to NOOP hours asleep.
Within ~15 min — good alignment on asleep minutes.
NOOP reads ~%1$d min more asleep than HC for this wake-day.
NOOP reads ~%1$d min less asleep than HC for this wake-day.
Allow Health Connect sleep stages or log WHOOP Sleep %% so phone nights show beside NOOP.
Each line is how many points that part moved Rest versus a typical 50. Approximate, not medical advice.
No nights with sleep data yet. Your ledger fills in as you wear the strap to bed.
Your need tonight: %1$s (physiology + recent nights; Effort can raise it). Slept − need builds the ledger.
Need starts near 8h for adults. Harder Effort can raise it a little. Short nights don’t lower the bar.
Still yesterday’s awake span — answers cover yesterday through now, until you sleep.
Hours slept roughly match what you usually need across %1$s.
About %1$s less than you usually need across %2$s. Extra sleep nights pay it back.
About %1$s more than you usually need across %2$s.
Per-night sleep balance: %1$d nights, net %2$s, scale ±%3$s
Optional photo stays on this phone. Age feeds Fitness Age and Vitality — never uploaded.
Local to this phone. It does not change your strap, training data, exports, or backups.

Counter ticks per step. Leave at 1.0 unless your steps run high. On a WHOOP 5/MG they can run very high (10× or more), so this goes up to 30. Walk a known 1,000 steps and divide NOOP\'s count by the real count to get your value.
For a WHOOP 4.0, which sends no step count: NOOP estimates steps from motion, calibrated to your phone. Tap to see how close it is and adjust it.
Steps estimate calibration. %1$s. Opens the calibration screen.
Default is 12-hour. Applies to Today, Sleep, Alarm, reports, and haptic clock buzz.
Looking for your WHOOP… radio link or the official app may be holding it.
Your strap is paired and sending data. Open Live for a real-time heart rate.
Connected. Finishing the secure pairing handshake…
Previously paired but not currently connected. Re-scan to reconnect.
No strap linked. Put your WHOOP nearby and tap Re-scan.
Alongside WHOOP app: open HR, R-R and battery only — no private bond.
Drops auto-retry with backoff. Pause stops retries.

Three stories, one home: Devices = strap BLE pair · Health Connect = phone health import · Bluetooth settings = OS radio. Cycle reminders on Cycle. Alarms on Sleep | Alarm. System alerts under Notifications.
Live heart rate + banked vital signs.
Sync now. Pulls your strap\'s stored history immediately, without waiting for the next automatic sync.
Sync now. A sync is already in progress.
Sync now. Connect your strap first.
Syncing now · pulling stored history (oldest first). Keep the strap nearby.
Connect your strap to sync its stored history. Until then, only imported data shows here.
Finishing the pairing handshake. Sync now becomes available once the strap is paired.
Ready · Sync now pulls stored history right away (not silent — you’ll see Syncing…).
Lab Book = your cuff/bloods · Health = strap vitals · Insights = journal experiments.
Your bloods, BP and body numbers. Kept private here.
The best-sourced number per metric, across your bands.
A wellness estimate from your habits, not a clinical biological age.
Live heart rate over time, %1$d beats per minute, zone %2$d
Live heart rate over time, no data
No tip yet — wear the strap while awake, or sync a scored day.
Historical vitals from your cached daily metrics.
Record or inspect the current stream
WHOOP 5.0/MG pairs with one app at a time. If a scan finds nothing, unpair it in the official WHOOP app and fully close that app, then Connect again.
Live link looks like an unworn 5AM sibling. Prefer your worn MG when both are bonded.
Scan and connect to start a live stream.
Steps from the strap this session. Train the step scale in Settings if the count feels off.
WHOOP 4 has no live @57 pedometer field. Daily steps use band gravity/IMU motion (est.) — not the phone pedometer.
Live heart rate is above. These tiles are banked or Lab Book only — full grid in Health Monitor.
Buzz your strap when these apps notify you. Everything runs on this device.
When on, NOOP taps your wrist for the apps you pick below, so you can leave your phone and still feel what matters.
Wrist delivery needs Notification Access so NOOP can read which apps notify you. Nothing leaves this device. Your choices are saved now and apply automatically once access is granted.
Tap your wrist for incoming phone calls and strict best-effort VoIP calls.
Uses the same quiet-hours and worn-only rules.
Needs Phone permission; NOOP never reads numbers or call logs.
Phone permission was denied, so phone-call buzzing is off.
Detects call-style notifications from known calling apps.
Fine-tune when alerts reach your wrist.
Skip alerts when the strap is off your wrist.

Also buzz for apps that aren\'t in the lists above (e.g. BeReal). Android doesn\'t let NOOP see every installed app, so this is how you cover the rest. Can be chatty; quiet hours and \"only when worn\" still apply.

Optional phone notifications, off by default. These arrive after your strap syncs and NOOP scores the data, so they land soon after, not the exact second you wake or finish a workout. Everything is worked out on this phone.
After last night is processed, a notification with your Charge and Rest. Posts once a day, after your strap has synced the night.
When a new workout syncs in, a notification with its Effort, duration and average heart rate. Shows up after the session reaches NOOP on the next sync.
Hour and minute steppers. Double tap plus or minus to change.
